mnemonics.title: GAN Training Steps
mnemonics.content: To remember GAN training: 'D Discriminates Real vs Fake first, then G Generates to Fool D. Alternate and repeat until equilibrium.'

mnemonics.title: DCGAN Architecture
mnemonics.content: DCGAN记忆: 'G uses Transpose Conv (upscale), BatchNorm, ReLU, Tanh output. D uses Stride Conv (downscale), BatchNorm, LeakyReLU, Sigmoid output.'

mnemonics.title: GAN Loss Function
mnemonics.content: Remember minimax: 'G wants D(G(z)) → 1 (fool D), D wants D(x) → 1 (real), D(G(z)) → 0 (fake). The log formula captures this adversarial game.'

acronyms.usage: A framework where two networks compete: Generator creates fakes, Discriminator judges them.
GAN using deep convolutional architectures for image generation tasks.
GAN that conditions on additional info (labels) to generate class-specific samples.
GAN using Wasserstein distance for more stable training.
Metric to evaluate quality of generated images by measuring distance between distributions.
Symmetric measure of difference between two probability distributions used in original GAN loss.

visualTips.visualization: Think of it as a counterfeiter (Generator) vs police (Discriminator). The counterfeiter tries to make perfect fake bills, police tries to detect fakes. Both improve through competition.
Imagine a balance scale. When Discriminator is too strong (falls), Generator gradients vanish (light). When balanced, both learn effectively. Visualize oscillating needle seeking center.

Imagine latent space as a continuous landscape where each point represents a potential generated image. Moving smoothly through this space creates gradual, meaningful transitions between generated outputs.
Picture a multimodal distribution (multiple peaks) collapsing to single peak - like a colorful rainbow collapsing to just one color. The Generator finds one winning mode and stays there.

analogies.analogy: Like a student (Generator) taking an exam where the teacher (Discriminator) keeps changing questions. Both get smarter over time - student learns to handle varied questions, teacher creates harder ones.
Think of the Generator as an art forger studying great paintings to create convincing fakes, while learning what makes originals valuable.
The Discriminator is like an art expert examining paintings - the more real and fake art they see, the better they become at spotting forgeries.
Imagine two poker players who have studied each other so thoroughly that neither can gain advantage - they reach a stalemate where bluffs work exactly 50% of the time.
